seoTitle: How to Assign Tasks on Trello
metaDescription: Delegate Trello cards to team members using the card back, drag-and-drop avatars, or keyboard shortcuts, and manage checklist ownership.
intro: Clear task ownership keeps a project moving, and Trello gives you a few different ways to assign work depending on how fast you need to move. This guide covers the standard card-back method, quick drag-and-drop, and keyboard shortcuts for assigning tasks.

steps.heading: Open the card back
steps.body: Click your target project card to expand its full details panel.

steps.heading: Add a member
steps.body: In the right sidebar, click the Members button and select the person you want to assign.

steps.heading: Try the drag-and-drop shortcut
steps.body: Hover over the team avatar row in the top header, then click and drag an avatar directly onto a card to assign it instantly.

steps.heading: Use keyboard hotkeys
steps.body: Hover your mouse over a card and press A to open the member menu, or press Space to instantly assign yourself.

steps.heading: Assign checklist items
steps.body: Create a checklist and click the small member icon next to a subtask row to assign ownership at a granular level.

steps.heading: Unassign a member if needed
steps.body: Click an avatar on the card back and select 'Remove from Card' to instantly unassign a user.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to assign a card to myself?
faq.answer: Hover your mouse over the card and press the Space key to instantly assign yourself without opening the card.

faq.question: Can I assign more than one person to a card?
faq.answer: Yes, Trello supports multi-member assignments for cards that need cross-functional collaboration.

faq.question: Do assigned members get notified automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, Trello's notification engine automatically alerts assigned members about card updates and workflow shifts.

faq.question: Can I assign ownership to individual checklist items?
faq.answer: Yes, click the member icon next to a specific subtask row to assign ownership at the checklist level.
